From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Italian actor

| Cesare Zoppetti | |
| --- | --- |
| Zoppetti in *[What Scoundrels Men Are!](/wiki/What_Scoundrels_Men_Are!_(1932_film) "What Scoundrels Men Are! (1932 film)")* (1932) | |
| Born | (1876-01-01)1 January 1876  [Genoa](/wiki/Genoa "Genoa"), [Liguria](/wiki/Liguria "Liguria")   Italy |
| Died | 15 March 1940(1940-03-15) (aged 64)  Rome, [Lazio](/wiki/Lazio "Lazio")  Italy |
| Occupation | Actor |
| Years active | 1930–1940 (film) |

**Cesare Zoppetti** (1 January 1876 – 15 March 1940) was an Italian [stage](/wiki/Stage_actor "Stage actor") and [film actor](/wiki/Film_actor "Film actor"). Zoppetti was a prolific film actor of the 1930s, appearing in nearly fifty productions between 1930 and his death in 1940 including the comedy *[What Scoundrels Men Are!](/wiki/What_Scoundrels_Men_Are!_(1932_film) "What Scoundrels Men Are! (1932 film)")* (1932).[[1]](#cite_note-1)
